'''Adjectives''' in Aelaki originate from stative verb stems. They retain much of the verbal morphological system, including evidentiality marking, while also obeying noun-like agreement rules.

== Morphology ==

Adjectives undergo the same templatic morphological processes as other parts of speech in Aelaki:

* '''[[Comparative]]''': Formed by reduplication of C₂. Example: ''koran'' "bright" → ''kororan'' "brighter"
* '''[[Superlative]]''': Formed by umlaut (vowel fronting). Example: ''koran'' "bright" → ''kœrœn'' "brightest"
* '''[[Negative]]''': Formed by /f/-infix. Example: ''koran'' "bright" → ''korfan'' "not bright"

== Agreement ==

Adjectives must agree with their head noun in both '''gender''' and '''number'''. This agreement is obligatory and applies in all syntactic positions.

== Evidentiality ==

Unlike adjectives in many natural languages, Aelaki adjectives retain a full eight-way TAM (tense-aspect-mood) / evidentiality system inherited from their stative verb origins:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Suffix !! Evidentiality !! Notes
|-
| ''-shë'' || Visual || Speaker saw it directly
|-
| ''-ro'' || Testimony || Speaker heard it from someone
|-
| ''-mu'' || Inferred || Speaker deduced it
|-
| ''-ya'' || Hopeful || Speaker hopes/wishes it
|-
| ''go-'' + ''-shë'' || Hodiernal visual || Seen today
|-
| ''go-'' + ''-ro'' || Hodiernal testimony || Heard today
|-
| ''go-'' + ''-mu'' || Hodiernal inferred || Inferred today
|-
| ''go-'' + ''-ya'' || Hodiernal hopeful || Hoped today
|}

'''Note:''' The prefix ''goki-'' is '''never''' used with adjectives.

== Example Paradigm ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Form !! Gloss
|-
| ''koran'' || bright ([[Positive]])
|-
| ''kororan'' || brighter ([[Comparative]])
|-
| ''kœrœn'' || brightest ([[Superlative]])
|-
| ''korfan'' || not bright ([[Negative]])
|}

[[Category:parts of speech]]
